title: IAM, Bucket Policies, Ownership, Access Points, and Public Access · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for iam, bucket policies, ownership, access points, and public access and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for least-privilege S3 authorization proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.
Enforce Block Public Access and bucket-owner-enforced Object Ownership while explaining what each does and does not authorize.
Derive tenant-safe keys and list prefixes server-side and bind allowed actions and conditions without trusting user-supplied policy fragments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 3640 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove explicit-deny-aware least privilege, public block, ownership, and tenant scope; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Review effective permissions with policy analysis, simulation, denied changed cases, temporary credentials, and CloudTrail evidence.
